selectionMethod: Canonical GitHub repositories ranked by stargazers_count, filtered for European legal registration, foundation governance, or strong documented European origin, then checked for recent pushed_at activity.

entries.name: n8n
entries.description: Workflow automation platform for connecting APIs, SaaS tools, internal systems, and AI workflows with self-hosting support.

origin.evidence: n8n's official imprint lists n8n GmbH in Berlin, Germany.

entries.whyItMatters: A high-adoption European automation platform for developers who need API orchestration, internal workflow tooling, and self-hostable automation.
entries.maintenanceSignal: Very active repository with same-day push activity during research.

entries.name: Godot Engine
entries.description: Free and open-source 2D and 3D game engine with broad platform support.

origin.evidence: Godot governance states that the Godot Foundation is a Dutch non-profit registered in the Netherlands.

entries.whyItMatters: One of the most visible European-governed open-source developer tools, useful for game development, simulations, education, and interactive tooling.
entries.maintenanceSignal: Recent push activity and public governance with community-driven technical decision making.

entries.name: Home Assistant
entries.description: Open-source home automation platform focused on local control and privacy.

origin.evidence: The Open Home Foundation owns and protects Home Assistant and lists Swiss registration identifier CHE-416.988.952.

entries.whyItMatters: A major European-governed IoT platform for developers building local-first automations, integrations, and privacy-preserving smart-home systems.
entries.maintenanceSignal: Same-day push activity and a large, active ecosystem of integrations and related projects.

entries.name: Elasticsearch
entries.description: Distributed search and analytics engine used for search, observability, and security workloads.

origin.evidence: Elastic N.V. is registered with a legal address in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

entries.whyItMatters: A major European-rooted search infrastructure project for developers building search, log analytics, vector retrieval, and operational data systems.
entries.maintenanceSignal: Same-day push activity and long-running enterprise maintenance.

entries.name: Grafana
entries.description: Open observability and data visualization platform for metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerts.

origin.evidence: data.europa.eu identifies Grafana Labs as a Sweden use case and notes that the Grafana project was started by Torkel Odegaard.

entries.whyItMatters: A default choice for European observability stacks, especially when teams need dashboards across Prometheus, Loki, Elasticsearch, Postgres, and other sources.
entries.maintenanceSignal: Same-day push activity and a large public contributor ecosystem.

nearMisses.name: Netdata
nearMisses.reasonNotTopFive: Very highly starred and founded by a Greek engineer, but current official privacy/legal materials identify Netdata, Inc. as a Delaware company with a San Francisco principal place of business, so it was not treated as Europe-primary in this pass.

nearMisses.name: Strapi
nearMisses.reasonNotTopFive: Strong French origin and Paris presence, but current terms identify Strapi, Inc. as a US company. Kept as a medium-confidence candidate rather than a top Europe-primary entry.
